item.title: Let's diffuse the situation
item.description: Tinkering with UNets to learn how diffusion models work

item.title: MiniGPT: A Minimalist Implementation of GPT-2
item.description: You can build a transformer too!

item.title: Did somebody say startup?
item.description: You miss all the shots you don’t take

item.title: Hack a startup - Gumbo (Post is WiP)
item.description: Taking a dive, I’m meant for this.

item.title: [ Placement Advice ] Probably.
item.description: The hoops to jump through for a job after undergrad at CET

I’m Abhishek Sankar, currently pursuing my MS in Artificial Intelligence and Innovation at Carnegie Mellon University. I previously worked a software engineer at Providence, where I got to work on interesting problems in healthcare. I’m passionate about building products that solve real-world problems. Small ones and the not so small ones. I’ve been a part of a few hackathons and have won a few of them. I’m currently working on Sebastian Raschka’s book Build a Large Language Model, where I’m learning about the internals of GPT-2 and GPT-3.

Abhishek Sankar Pittsburgh, PA (+1) 412.909.9156 abhisheksankar@cmu.edu abhisheksankar.com LinkedIn GitHub Education Carnegie Mellon University • Pittsburgh, PA Master of Science • Artificial Intelligence and Innovation • GPA: 4.0 Expected graduation - May 2026 College of Engineering Trivandrum • Trivandrum, India Bachelor of Technology • Computer Science & Engineering • GPA: 8.99/10 Graduated - July 2021 Skills Programming Languages: Python, Java, JavaScript Frameworks: PyTorch, FastAPI, React, TypeScript, Azure Work Experience Providence Global LLP – Senior Software Engineer Hyderabad, India
